title: Post Malone Teases New Double Album
author: Abby Jones
summary:
Between falling down onstage, modeling for Skims, and performing at the Grammys, Post Malone has still had enough time to make new music — a double album of new music, in fact! During his set at Indianapolis' March Madness Music Festival on Saturday, Posty told the crowd: "Ladies and gentlemen, we are currently working on two albums. It just so happens — I've been doing this for maybe 11 years. And I have kind of run out of shit to say, but I think we made some really cool shit, so I'm really excited for y'all crazy motherfuckers to hear it. There's gonna be a fuckton of songs, so hopefully there's something for everybody there."

The post
full_text:
Between falling down onstage, modeling for Skims, and performing at the Grammys, Post Malone has still had enough time to make new music — a double album of new music, in fact! During his set at Indianapolis' March Madness Music Festival on Saturday, Posty told the crowd: "Ladies and gentlemen, we are currently working on two albums. It just so happens — I've been doing this for maybe 11 years. And I have kind of run out of shit to say, but I think we made some really cool shit, so I'm really excited for y'all crazy motherfuckers to hear it. There's gonna be a fuckton of songs, so hopefully there's something for everybody there."

Post Malone also shared a photo on Instagram of a whiteboard with presumed album title The Eternal Buzz written at the top, along with "Disc 1" and "Disc 2." Under each "disc" are spaces for twenty tracks — 40 tracks total — with song titles left blank. "Coming soon," reads the caption. 

The Eternal Buzz will follow up Post's 2024 country-pivot album F-1 Trillion, and some fans are hoping the new album sees him veer back closer towards hip-hop. Last Friday, Post's "Sunflower" collaborator Swae Lee released his new album Same Difference, and Post features on its final track "Take My Heart"; an early version of the trap-pop song has been floating around online for a few years now.
